title: Living in Boulder Vlog 4 - 2 - 2017 Still doing Area Dimensions

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on April 3, 2017, giving a casual update on his day: working on his website building downloadable tables for race distances and circles, troubleshooting mobile display issues, trying to monetize his site (only $230 total earned), going to lunch with his mom, and planning to study libertarian content online to rebut it.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Frustrated that his ad-heavy site earns almost nothing despite heavy work; skeptical of claims that two million people make money from websites
Views them as enemies funded by rich billionaires to spread 'mind-fucking' propaganda; suspects prominent libertarian voices are paid shills
Acknowledges his site looks broken on mobile and needs to fix it, but is deferring it
Wants to finish his website before focusing on other things like learning ad monetization

entities: Jake's (restaurant, likely 'Jake and Telly's' or similar nearby)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was too lazy to do a vlog yesterday because I was working until 2 AM and then got really tired and went to sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on attaching or creating files to download for a Q&A.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to make two tables, one for race distances and another for circles, because combining them didn't fit right.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm creating files for the circles, but it's more intricate than I planned, so it's taking longer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My brother is helping with my motorbike, and my mom is handling selling my car, so I don't have to do anything.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: I'm trying to figure out why my website isn't making money, even though I've only made $230 total from it.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: My website is loaded with ads, and I'm listening to ad tutorials while working on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm reading about experiments and guides to monetize my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to watch videos and work on my website while riding my bike indoors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had lunch with my mom at Jake's Tweeter yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I finished a page on my website and showed my mom the tables I was working on at the restaurant.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: I noticed my website looks weird on mobile, with inconsistent text sizes and table widths.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I called Adobe for help with mobile design, but they don't have programs in Creative Cloud to fix it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I suspect libertarians are all paid shills and plan to study them to prove it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I watched a video about libertarians for two and a half hours and took notes, but lost them accidentally.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to target libertarians because they're funded by billionaires pushing misleading ideas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe diverting resources to bicycles for everyone could be a better system.

transcript.text: Hey, I was too lazy to do a vlog yesterday, because I was working until like 2 and then I just got really tired and had to go to sleep.
So I'll do the one for April 2nd, which is today's April 3rd at 11.50am.
I've just been working here on my thing, attaching or creating the files to download QMZ.
And yeah, I figured I'd do this for now.
The thing's dragging on.
I decided to make two, I'm like looking at myself, let's minimize this.
I made two tables, one for the race distances and then another one for the circles.
I was experimenting with having one table for both, but it doesn't fit right, so with two, yeah, that'll work.
And so I'm creating the files for the circles.
Yeah, this is more intricate than I planned on it being, so it's dragging on, but whatever, I don't care.
My brother offered to buy my motorbike and my mom's doing everything to sell my car, so I don't have to do anything.
So it's cool, I'm just going to keep working on my website.
I wanted to figure out why my website's not making any money.
Like, I was looking on some blog posts, and she's like, yeah, two million people make money on their websites.
I'm like, really?
Because all I do is work on my website.
I've made 230 bucks on my website total.
I make like zero pennies a day.
And again, it's loaded with ads.
So I'm listening to the AdSense tutorial right now and reading about experiments.
You do experiments with ads to make money with it.
And then I'll read through the other stuff.
And I'll just, I think I'll read.
I'll have my computer read to me and then watch all the videos while I'm working on this.
And then once I get through the whole thing, you know, that'll be like my introduction.
I'm getting excited about it.
But, and then I'll go, when I'm riding my bike on the inside, wherever that is, I'll do that then and be able to look at the screen and stuff.
Big old screen, my TV.
And I'll learn it once and for all.
But I'm not going to focus on that.
I want to finish my website.
But, yeah, so, anyway, was that all?
Oh, yeah, so yesterday I got up and I was working a little bit.
And then my mom called me and she's like, hey, I'll take you to lunch.
So she took me out to lunch at Che Tui Chinese Restaurant just a block away, a couple blocks away.
That was cool.
I had some leftovers that I took back here.
I just had.
Oh, no, that was last night.
And so, yeah, so here I'm making my links here.
Will I finish the page today?
To finish the, maybe I'll finish the two tables.
I was showing my mom at the restaurant yesterday how it was working on my cell phone.
It works fucking great on my cell phone.
I'll do a demo later.
So, yeah, I was looking at the graphics and the stuff on my AdSense for the first time.
And that was pretty interesting because it can show in real time.
But it's actually looking at my website in real time.
And then it shows, you know, how much mobile.
And it's like 30% mobile.
Like, I need to get my website looking good mobile.
My website looks weird mobile.
Like, and I call the Adobe people and they don't have any programs on the Creative Cloud that I have that helps with mobile yet.
But he told me what it was, but I'll figure it out later.
But I guess there's tutorials and there's these other programs you use to make it so that your website works with mobile.
Because, like, my website does weird shit.
I'll show you.
Oh, that is if my website loads.
That's weird.
It's not.
It usually loads right away.
My internet sucks here.
Like that.
Like, why is that word bigger than that word?
Why are those words bigger than those words?
And, like, why aren't these tables taking up the whole width of the screen?
And why do some of them take up the whole width and others not?
So I was hoping that they would figure it out.
They'd work out all the bugs themselves.
But whatever.
Anyway, that kind of stuff.
Oh, and I wanted to listen to – I need to study the libertarians because I'm going to make them my enemies, tear them apart.
I suspect they're all paid stooges, and I'll prove it.
So that's what I'll do.
I watched that guy.
He talked about the fall of Rome for two and a half hours, and I wrote down points to make.
It was, like, 30 points, but I forgot to copy it, and I accidentally backed out of the page.
I was like, no.
So from now on, whenever I make the points, you have to copy it, and then paste it some notes right away because I'm the kind of person who I would do it, and then I'd come and do it later.
But anyway, he had another one about the parallels of the fall of Rome, and I know he's got his stupid libertarian talking points in there.
So I'll just watch that one.
But anyway, yeah, because I need to go after the libertarians because they're – those rich billionaires, they're diverting their money to the wrong stuff, to just this weird mind-bucking shit.
To try to say, oh, yeah, riches be good.
And so if you can divert it to bike school for everybody, then bam!
The new economic system.
Anyways, let's cut it off.
So no background, just me here.
All right, peace.
Peace.
